mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to yield a positive return probability above 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which increases near-term corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive production trends produce negative forward returns in > 50% of cases during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when the inflation threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor market absorption that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative return probability above 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals immediate supply-side contraction that reduces near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative production shocks fail to predict negative returns with a probability exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing.
